title: Incremental Strategy
description: Incremental Strategy for dbt-trino. Pick one of: 'append', 'delete+insert', 'merge', 'overwrite_existing_partitions'. NOTE: 'overwrite_existing_partitions' requires a custom dbt macro in your project and is not shipped by the DJ extension. 'merge' requires the target table to use Iceberg format in dbt-trino. When in doubt, use 'delete+insert' with a partition column as unique_key.
markdownDescription:
**Incremental Strategy (dbt-trino)**

| Strategy | When to use | Caveat |
|---|---|---|
| `append` | Fast insert-only, no dedup | Upstream must guarantee no duplicates |
| `delete+insert` | Partition-safe upsert, **safe default** | Requires `unique_key` (auto-derived from partitions) |
| `merge` | Row-level upsert on a primary key | **dbt-trino requires Iceberg format** |
| `overwrite_existing_partitions` | Re-drop & rewrite only touched partitions | **Requires a custom dbt macro in your project.** The DJ extension does NOT ship this strategy. If your project does not define `get_incremental_overwrite_existing_partitions_sql` (or the equivalent adapter dispatch macro), use `delete+insert` instead. |

anyOf.description: Append: insert new rows without de-duplication. Fastest strategy, but upstream must guarantee no duplicates in the new slice. No unique_key needed.
anyOf.markdownDescription: **`append`** — insert new rows without dedup. Fastest; upstream must guarantee no duplicates.

anyOf.description: Delete+insert: delete rows matching unique_key, then insert. The safe default for date-partitioned data — unique_key is auto-derived from the model's partition column(s) when omitted.
anyOf.markdownDescription: **`delete+insert`** — partition-safe upsert. If `unique_key` is omitted, DJ auto-derives it from the model's partition column(s).

type.description: Delete rows matching unique_key, then insert.

unique_key.description: Override the unique key(s) to use for delete+insert. Defaults to the model's partition column when omitted.

anyOf.description: Merge: row-level upsert on unique_key. IMPORTANT: for dbt-trino the target table must use Iceberg format — set materialization.format to 'iceberg' or set the project var storage_type to 'iceberg'. If you are on Delta Lake / Hive, use 'delete+insert' instead.
anyOf.markdownDescription:
**`merge`** — row-level upsert on `unique_key`.

> **WARNING:** dbt-trino only supports `merge` on **Iceberg** tables. Set `materialization.format: "iceberg"` or the project var `storage_type: iceberg`. On Delta Lake / Hive use `delete+insert` instead.

type.description: Row-level upsert on unique_key. Requires Iceberg in dbt-trino.

unique_key.description: The unique key(s) to use for merging.

merge_exclude_columns.description: The columns to exclude when merging.

merge_update_columns.description: The columns to update when merging.

anyOf.description: Overwrite existing partitions: drop and rewrite only the partitions present in the new slice. REQUIRES a custom dbt macro in your project (e.g. get_incremental_overwrite_existing_partitions_sql). The DJ extension does NOT ship this macro and dbt-trino does NOT provide it natively. If your project does not define this macro, use 'delete+insert' with a partition column as unique_key — it produces equivalent behavior for daily/monthly partitioned models.
anyOf.markdownDescription:
**`overwrite_existing_partitions`** — drop & rewrite only partitions in the new slice.

> **WARNING — CUSTOM MACRO REQUIRED.** This strategy is NOT shipped by the DJ extension and is NOT part of vanilla dbt-trino. Your dbt project must define the dispatch macro (typically `get_incremental_overwrite_existing_partitions_sql`) for it to compile.
>
> **Don't have the macro?** Use `{ "type": "delete+insert" }` instead — if a partition column exists, DJ auto-fills `unique_key` and the behavior is equivalent for partition-aligned daily/monthly incrementals.

type.description: Overwrite only the partitions in the new slice. REQUIRES a custom macro in your dbt project — prefer 'delete+insert' if you do not have one.

unique_key.description: Optional override for the unique key(s). Defaults to the model's partition column when omitted.
